talk_title: DPP4 as a novel checkpoint for CD8 T cells in glioblastoma

biomarkers.name: DPP4 expression in CD8 T cells
biomarkers.finding: Higher DPP4 levels correlate with terminal exhaustion phenotype

biomarkers.name: TOX expression
biomarkers.finding: TOX upregulated in exhausted CD8 T cells; reduced after DPP4 inhibition

models.name: GPR model
models.genotype_or_key_features: human GBM cells implanted in mouse brain

clinical_data.comparator: standard of care without DPP4 inhibitors

clinical_data.population: orthotopic GBM mice treated with sitagliptin

MOA_summary: DPP4 acts as an immunometabolic checkpoint on CD8 T cells; its inhibition by sitagliptin restores mitochondrial respiration and glycolysis, enhances proliferation, cytotoxic mediator production, and degranulation, partly via up‑regulation of GAD1‑mediated TCA cycle flux, leading to improved anti‑GBM activity.
key_findings: Retrospective analysis of Medicare data showed GBM patients on DPP4 inhibitors had significantly longer survival than matched controls.
CD8 T cells in human GBM express high DPP4, especially terminally exhausted (TOX‑high) subsets.
Pharmacologic or genetic DPP4 inhibition increased CD8 proliferation, cytokine production, and cytotoxicity in vitro.
DPP4 inhibition up‑regulated mitochondrial respiratory capacity and glycolysis, linked to GAD1 activation.
In orthotopic GBM mouse models, sitagliptin prolonged survival, an effect abolished by CD8 depletion.
Global DPP4 knockout mice recapitulated the pharmacologic phenotype, confirming target specificity.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: DPP4 inhibition had minimal impact on myeloid chemotaxis function, suggesting cell‑type selective effects.

combination_strategies.rationale: DPP4 inhibition restores T cell function that may synergize with PD‑1 blockade
combination_strategies.phenotype: potential additive tumor control (not yet tested)

open_questions: What are the long‑term effects of chronic DPP4 inhibition on T cell memory formation?
Can on‑demand DPP4 inhibition be achieved to avoid systemic metabolic effects?
